title: phacelia divaricata subsp. wrangeliana
intro: Phacelia divaricata subsp. wrangeliana, also known as Wrangel's phacelia, is a plant species belonging to the borage family and native to the Russian Far East and northeastern Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Wrangel's phacelia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Wrangel's phacelia can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have also been studied for their potential use in pharmaceutical applications.
